title: Woodside Output Hit by Weather Disruptions but LNG Megaprojects Stay on Track
body: Oops, something went wrong Woodside Energy reported a decline in first-quarter production, weighed down by seasonal weather disruptions, even as the company maintained high operational reliability and continued advancing its major LNG and growth projects. The Australian oil and gas producer delivered production of 45.2 million barrels of oil equivalent (MMboe) in the first quarter of 2026, down 8% from the previous quarter, primarily due to cyclone-related impacts across Western Australia. Despite the drop in output, Woodside emphasized strong operational performance, with key assets including Sangomar, Pluto LNG, and the North West Shelf operating at or near 99% reliability. title: Iran War Triggers Helium Shock Threatening Global Chip Supply
body: Oops, something went wrong Last month, QatarEnergy declared force majeure on long-term liquefied natural gas (LNG) contracts to buyers in Europe and Asia following severe damage to its Ras Laffan production facilities by Iran’s missile strikes. This disrupted ~20% of global LNG supply and triggered a sharp spike in natural gas prices. Thankfully, gas prices have pulled back to pre-war levels, with natural gas markets coping remarkably well with the loss, thanks to expected volumes to be delivered to the market outweighing current and expected reductions over the next few years. It’s all helping to tame natural gas prices . But the same cannot be said about another vital industrial gas: Helium. title: Ecopetrol (EC) Ascends While Market Falls: Some Facts to Note
body: Oops, something went wrong Ecopetrol (EC) closed at $13.97 in the latest trading session, marking a +1.82% move from the prior day. The stock outperformed the S&P 500, which registered a daily loss of 0.49%. Elsewhere, the Dow saw a downswing of 0.05%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq depreciated by 0.9%. The stock of oil and natural gas exploration company has fallen by 8.41% in the past month, lagging the Oils-Energy sector's loss of 4.6% and the S&P 500's gain of 12.8%. Market participants will be closely following the financial results of Ecopetrol in its upcoming release. In that report, analysts expect Ecopetrol to post earnings of $0.43 per share.
